data.content: The tombstone shows `libmonosgen-2.0.so` frames with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. I'll try to fetch debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server and symbolicate the addresses.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-1a2e975ee8544b1497c7d6829279be34/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the ELF debug info format from MSDL
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  && echo "Downloaded debug symbols" || echo "Debug symbols not found at MSDL"

# Also try the binary itself
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "Downloaded binary" || echo "Binary not found at MSDL"
arguments.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The frames are at offsets:

- #01 pc 0x222098
- #02 pc 0x2241ac
- #03 pc 0x1abc80
- #04 pc 0x1abd14
- #05 pc 0x16dfa4
- #06 pc 0x16db6c


- #07 pc 0x16de04

To get the actual function names and source locations, I need the debug symbols for this specific build. I can retrieve them from Microsoft's symbol server or use the `dotnet-symbol` tool, then apply `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to map these offsets to their corresponding symbols.

The symbol server URL format for ELF files on Linux uses the BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be` to construct the path. Let me work through the proper way to fetch and use these symbols.

But I'm realizing this tombstone might be sample data—the app name "MauiTest" suggests it could be synthetic rather than a real crash dump. I'll try a few different approaches to see which one works.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-1a2e975ee8544b1497c7d6829279be34/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the ELF debug info format from MSDL
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  && echo "Downloaded debug symbols" || echo "Debug symbols not found at MSDL"

# Also try the binary itself
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "Downloaded binary" || echo "Binary not found at MSDL"

arguments.command:
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-1a2e975ee8544b1497c7d6829279be34/symbols"
DBG="$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg"

# Symbolicate all libmonosgen-2.0.so frames
# Addresses from tombstone: #01-#07
ADDRS="0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04"

for addr in $ADDRS; do
  echo "=== pc $addr ==="
  addr2line -C -f -e "$DBG" "$addr"
  echo
done

data.reasoningText:
The files are valid ELF files for ARM aarch64 with matching BuildIDs. The .dbg file has debug_info. Now I need to use addr2line to symbolicate the addresses. Since these are aarch64 binaries and I'm on x86_64, I might need a cross-architecture addr2line. Let me check if the regular addr2line can handle it - actually, addr2line typically works with DWARF debug info regardless of architecture since it's just reading debug data.

data.content:
Here's the symbolicated backtrace for the `libmonosgen-2.0.so` frames:

```
#00 pc 0x6ae1c   libc.so                (abort+164)
#01 pc 0x222098  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast          (icall.c:6244)
#02 pc 0x2241ac  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw      (icall-def.h:202)
#03 pc 0x1abc80  libmonosgen-2.0.so     do_icall                                       (interp.c:2457)
#04 pc 0x1abd14  libmonosgen-2.0.so     get_interp_to_native_trampoline                (interp.c:1732)
#05 pc 0x16dfa4  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c)
#06 pc 0x16db6c  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c:506)
#07 pc 0x16de04  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c:528)
#08 pc 0x307c    <anonymous>            (managed/JIT code)
```

**Summary:** The crash originated from a type-checking failure (`handle_isinst` in `type-checking.c`) that escalated through the Mono interpreter (`do_icall`) into `Environment.FailFast`, which called `abort()`. The abort message `"not combatible"` and the memory dump showing `"some intentional crash on startup"` suggest this was a deliberate fail-fast triggered by a compatibility check during app startup.
